text: Hearing the repeated advice to call for help reminds me of the threads of support woven into society—structures to catch us when we fall. The stark, structured interior and the mix of people and public service signs make me feel simultaneously reassured and a bit isolated, as if the potential for crisis always lingers just below the surface in such ordered places. It's comforting that help is just a number away, but the need to repeat it suggests a hidden anxiety. I stand between possibility and vulnerability, held by the architecture of care and caution.
